title: The Room You Are Already Standing In
subtitle: A visualisation for seeing and feeling the wealth that already exists in your life rather than the wealth still to come
excerpt: A short visualisation for the Day 78 theme of feeling gratitude for present wealth. Instead of projecting forward, the scene stays in the present. You look at the room you are standing in (the life you are already living) and let it register as the abundance it is.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Present-tense gratitude visualisation of this kind appears across therapeutic and coaching traditions. No single source is credited.

content.text: A short visualisation for the Day 78 theme of feeling gratitude for present wealth. Instead of projecting forward, the scene stays here. You look at what already exists in your life and let it register as the abundance it is.
Sit somewhere quiet. Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Two or three slow breaths.
You are in a room. It is your room, wherever you actually are. Open your eyes for a moment if it helps. Look at it. Then close them again.

Notice what is in the room. The specific object: the chair, the heating, the floor. These are all things that are paid for or owned. The access to a room at all is a form of wealth that many people do not have. The safety of the room is wealth.

Now expand the picture beyond the room. The accounts that are funded. The income that is flowing, even if not as much as you want. The investments or property that are working in the background right now. The relationships and the freedom to choose today's work. These are all part of the picture of what already exists.

See the whole picture at once. Not compared to a future version. Not compared to someone else. Just the thing itself, in front of you. Let it be enough for this moment. Let it register as real and substantial, because it is.
Sit with the picture for a moment. Then open your eyes.
This reading is original to homemade.education. The image practice of looking around in gratitude shows up in many therapy and coaching forms. No single source is claimed here.
